mission.title: metal3-io: Typo in bootMACAddress causes non-obvious failures
mission.description: Typo in bootMACAddress causes non-obvious failures. Community-reported issue.

steps.title: Identify metal3-io troubleshoot symptoms
steps.description:
Check for the issue in your metal3-io deployment:
```bash
kubectl get pods -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io -n metal3-io --tail=100 | grep -i error
```
Look for errors or warnings in the logs that may indicate the issue.

steps.title: Review metal3-io configuration
steps.description:
Inspect the relevant metal3-io configuration:
```bash
kubectl get all -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io
kubectl get configmap -n metal3-io -l app.kubernetes.io/part-of=metal3-io
```
If someone accidentally writes `bootMacAddress` instead of `bootMACAddress` (or any other similar typo) we skip creating the ironic port for that node and silently ignore the wrong key containing the MAC.

steps.title: Apply the fix for Typo in bootMACAddress causes non-obvious failures
steps.description:
This causes stricter client-side validation against the schema
so that problems like typos in the user-provided BMH yaml can
be caught earlier and report a clear error message.
```yaml
$ kubectl apply -f bmhosts_crs.yaml
secret/node-0-bmc-secret unchanged
error: error validating "bmhosts_crs.yaml": error validating data: ValidationError(BareMetalHost.spec): unknown field "bootMacAddress" in io.metal3.v1alpha1.BareMetalHost.spec; if you choose to ignore these errors, turn validation off with --validate=false
```

steps.title: Confirm Typo in bootMACAddress causes non-obvious failures is resolved
steps.description:
Verify the fix by checking that the original error no longer occurs:
```bash
kubectl logs -l app.kubernetes.io/name=metal3-io -n metal3-io --tail=50 --since=5m
kubectl get events -n metal3-io --sort-by='.lastTimestamp' | tail -10
```
Confirm that the issue symptoms are gone.

resolution.summary:
This causes stricter client-side validation against the schema
so that problems like typos in the user-provided BMH yaml can
be caught earlier and report a clear error message.
codeSnippets:
$ kubectl apply -f bmhosts_crs.yaml
secret/node-0-bmc-secret unchanged
error: error validating "bmhosts_crs.yaml": error validating data: ValidationError(BareMetalHost.spec): unknown field "bootMacAddress" in io.metal3.v1alpha1.BareMetalHost.spec; if you choose to ignore these errors, turn validation off with --validate=false

prerequisites.description: A running Kubernetes cluster with metal3-io installed or the issue environment reproducible.
